original: then struggle to remove your [pc.lowerGarments], then
you splay your legs, then you push.
Next
You reach for the nearest medkit before hurrying to your quarters to set yourself on your bed. You get as comfortable as you can in your hurry,
You flag down the nearest civilian and tell them the situation, and that you’re in some pretty dire need of medical assistance. You find a safe place to sit down,
You’re in a less-than-ideal environment for this to be happening, but you don’t have a lot of time to be complaining. You find a safe place to sit yourself down,

You lack a lot of conscious input in the entire procedure; your body knows what it needs to do and you don’t try to hold yourself back just because you’re not in a hospital or a nursery. Although the situation is no less urgent than any other pregnancy, the whole ordeal is otherwise quite smooth and it’s not nearly as painful as you were expecting – mostly because you’re not pushing out a very small Frostwyrm, but because you’re pushing out an egg the size of [pc.aOriginalRace] baby.

When the scenario is over, you aren’t left with a kicking, screaming, breathing bundle of joy, but a very large, sturdy egg. It’s pure white all over, with no distinguishing markings around it. In a way, you’re kind of relieved to know that your crossbreeding with Nykke resulted in a perfectly normal Frostwyrm egg, and not something smaller or differently-shaped, and the baby inside it is likely just as healthy.

The last concern is answered when you hear the telltale melodic sounds, still in your head: despite being physically separated from you, the kip inside is still psionically bonded with you enough to communicate in its wordless way.

You have lain?! you hear Nykke say suddenly, startling you. You never liked it when [frostwyrm.name] did that to you, and you were hoping Nykke wouldn’t make a habit of it too. And our egg is on its way?! But how? Are you not on our world? I know that your kind can travel the stars–

Nykke’s communication stops suddenly, then starts again once she’s collected herself. I will not pretend to understand the specifics, but you tell when and where to find our egg, and I will fly there with a speed that [frostwyrm.name] wishes she could manage.

When the transit ship arrives, you load the heavy egg into its crib. It was built for a living creature, but it houses your egg sturdily. When the ship leaves, you reach out to Nykke, and inform her that the egg should arrive before the end of the day – or, uh, before Light’s Fall.
With all of that squared away, you shakily bring yourself to your [pc.feet]. You’re a good amount of weight lighter, but that still knocked all the energy out of you. You should... probably lie down.
Just as your thoughts were turning to your rotund and overbearing belly, you feel something in your loins, followed by a rush in your lower stomach. It’s time; the kip’s on its way right now.

Your movements have slowly begun to feel... sluggish. Like your body weighs a little more than it should. You’ve also been craving foods that are high in protein lately, and you’ve been drinking a lot more water than usual recently.\n\nYou consider the possibility that maybe you’re just craving foods and you’re more dehydrated than you realize, but you know that, so soon from your coupling with Nykke, it’s better to assume that you’re good and pregnant. Still, no need to panic yet; just keep doing what you’re doing. You’ll know for sure in a couple days.

Your stomach’s a little more bloated these past few days than usual, and your cravings for certain foods and tons of water haven’t stopped. When you press into your belly, the surface feels a lot tougher and firmer than you believe should be right.\n\nNo point in beating around the bush: you’re pregnant with Nykke’s kip. This is hardly your first rodeo, but you know to take it a little more cautiously regardless. No doubt that Nykke’s kip still retains all its hardy Frostwyrm heritage, but that’s no excuse to go wild. You’re eating for two, now.

Lately you’ve been feeling a little... different. Not in a bad way, exactly, but not always in a good way either. Your mood sometimes swings pretty wildly, from joyful and expressive, to angry and borderline destructive, to horny and lustful, to everything in between. But the words for those emotions don’t really do them justice; what you truly feel is much more... primeval. Raw. Unfocused. This may not be your first kip, but that doesn’t make it any easier for you to deal with. Your little passenger’s psionics are already so strong, and your connection with it is already so solid, that it’s nearly commandeering your own psyche and overriding you with its own. It wasn’t pleasant the first time, and it’s not pleasant this time. And all of that is on top of your growing paunch."+({var0}.isLactating()?"":" Your boobs also aren’t getting any larger, and you don’t feel like you’re about to start lactating, so you don’t even get the fun stuff out of this pregnancy.")+" It’s only been fifty days; you still have seventy more, thereabouts, until this thing is out of you.
context: <<POS:36657679>> <<VARS:var0=pc>> ParseText("Lately you’ve been feeling a little... different. Not in a bad way, exactly, but not always in a good way either. Your mood sometimes swings pretty wildly, from joyful and expressive, to angry and borderline destructive, to horny and lustful, to everything in between. But the words for those emotions don’t really do them justice; what you truly feel is much more... primeval. Raw. Unfocused. This may not be your first kip, but that doesn’t make it any easier for you to deal with. Your little passenger’s psionics are already so strong, and your connection with it is already so solid, that it’s nearly commandeering your own psyche and overriding you with its own. It wasn’t pleasant the first time, and it’s not pleasant this time. And all of that is on top of your growing paunch."+(pc.isLactating()?"":" Your boobs also aren’t getting any larger, and you don’t feel like you’re about to start lactating, so you don’t even get the fun stuff out of this pregnancy.")+" It’s only been fifty days; you still have seventy more, thereabouts, until this thing is out of you.")
